Patch Set 1:

Yangsheng, you are right, we don't print the registers.

Now the question is are they really that useful outside of a coredump? Because the generated coredump will have all the registers and much more.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> Yangsheng, you are right, we don't print the registers.
 > 
 > Now the question is are they really that useful outside of a
 > coredump? Because the generated coredump will have all the
 > registers and much more.

If have a way to collect register from vmcore. Of course, It is ok.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Yangsheng, you are right, we don't print the registers.
> 
> Now the question is are they really that useful outside of a coredump? Because the generated coredump will have all the registers and much more.

Sometimes from the customer sites there are no coredumps saved, in such cases printing the registers during LBUG could be useful to determine the cause of the crash.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Yangsheng, you are right, we don't print the registers.
> > 
> > Now the question is are they really that useful outside of a coredump? Because the generated coredump will have all the registers and much more.
> 
> Sometimes from the customer sites there are no coredumps saved, in such cases printing the registers during LBUG could be useful to determine the cause of the crash.

Is this just the case of a poorly implemented lbug where LASSERTF should have been used? Do you have some examples demonstrating this i na relatively universal way?

(I think we should strive to always have avilabiltiy of crashdumps, but I also know dark sites and other similar places exist where exfiltratign bg bulky binary data is complicated/impossible compared to whatever text/console logs)

Anyway, output register info is still a valuable options in such case.

parents.subject: LU-6142 mdd: Fix style issues for mdd_internal.h

commit.subject: LU-18445 libcfs: Remove msleep before panic for lbug
commit.message:
LU-18445 libcfs: Remove msleep before panic for lbug

If panic on lbug is set, do not sleep before calling
the panic() as it has very undesirable side effects.
Logs are getting rotated, racing threads continue moving
and get harder to understand what they were doing and
so on.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Fixes: 9f44a48b365 ("LU-17793 libcfs: fix objtool warning in lbug_with_loc()")
Change-Id: Idd49bda78c6441a11d3ea18cb053fd9755552604
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57010
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
